message:
perf(core): O(n^2) -> O(n log n) recursive CTE iteration

Recursive CTE iteration was rebuilding `seen_rows: BTreeSet` from the
full accumulated `all_rows` on every iteration. For an n-row CTE that
is n x (O(n) clone + O(log n) insert) = O(n^2) work. At n=1000 this
produced the 172x regression reported in benchmark_report_20260417_0132
(142 us in C SQLite vs 24.6 ms in FrankenSQLite).

Fix:
- Hoist `seen_rows` construction outside the iteration loop.
- Only materialize it for CTEs containing a UNION (non-ALL) arm;
  UNION ALL arms never need the set, so pure UNION ALL stays zero-alloc.
- Apply the change to both recursive-CTE evaluation paths in
  connection.rs (the general materialized path at ~38432 and the
  direct-eval-plan path at ~38580).

Repro test (crates/fsqlite-core/tests/recursive_cte_perf_repro.rs)
measures total query time at N=100/300/900 and asserts the scaling
ratio t900/t100 < 25x, which is decisively sub-quadratic (pre-fix the
ratio was >70x).

All 26 non-pre-existing recursive_cte tests pass. Two CTE tests are
failing on main (unrelated to this change) and continue to fail with
the fix applied; not regressed here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
